[India](/wiki/India "India") is a [federal](/wiki/Federalism "Federalism") [union](/wiki/Political_union "Political union") comprising 28 [states](/wiki/Federated_state "Federated state") and 8 [union territories](/wiki/Union_territory "Union territory"), for a total of 36 [subnational entities](/wiki/Subnational_entities "Subnational entities").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The states and union territories in turn are each subdivided into a total of around 800 [districts](/wiki/List_of_districts_in_India "List of districts in India"), which contain further [administrative subdivisions](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_India "Administrative divisions of India").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Under the Indian Constitution and laws, the states of India are self-governing administrative divisions, each having a [state government](/wiki/State_governments_of_India "State governments of India"). The legal power to manage affairs in each state is shared or *divided* between the particular state government on one hand and the national [union government](/wiki/Government_of_India "Government of India") on the other. The union territories are directly governed by the union government; no state level government (and thus no division of power) exists in these jurisdictions.[[a]](#cite_note-3)

## History

### 1876–1919

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d1/Political_Divisions_of_the_Indian_Empire%2C_1909.jpg/500px-Political_Divisions_of_the_Indian_Empire%2C_1909.jpg)](/wiki/File:Political_Divisions_of_the_Indian_Empire,_1909.jpg)

The administrative divisions of the Indian Empire in 1909

The [British Raj](/wiki/British_Raj "British Raj") was a very complex political entity consisting of various imperial divisions and states and territories of varying autonomy. At the time of its legal establishment in 1876, it was made up of 584 [constituent states](/wiki/Princely_state "Princely state") and the [directly ruled territories of the Crown](/wiki/Provinces_of_India "Provinces of India"). The entire empire was divided into provinces and agencies.

A [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_India "Provinces of India") consisted of territory under the direct rule of the [Emperor of India](/wiki/Emperor_of_India "Emperor of India") (who was also the [King of the United Kingdom and the Dominions](/wiki/King_of_the_United_Kingdom_and_the_Dominions "King of the United Kingdom and the Dominions")) and a few minor states, ruled by Indian princes under the suzerainty of the Emperor. A [Governor](/wiki/Governor "Governor") or [Lieutenant-Governor](/wiki/Lieutenant-Governor "Lieutenant-Governor") acted as representative of the [Emperor](/wiki/Emperor_of_India "Emperor of India") in that province and as [head of government](/wiki/Head_of_government "Head of government") of the directly ruled territories in the province.

The governor or lieutenant-governor also served as the Emperor's representative to the [constituent states](/wiki/Princely_state "Princely state") of the province. The first three of the lieutenant-governorships were territories annexed to India from other powers and temporarily governed by the erstwhile Bengal Presidency, before being made into their own separate provinces. [Agra](/wiki/Agra_Province "Agra Province") and [Bengal](/wiki/Bengal_Presidency "Bengal Presidency") were still considered *de jure* parts of the defunct Bengal Presidency for judicial and legal purposes. Agra was separated in 1878 and merged with [Oudh](/wiki/Oudh_Province "Oudh Province").

The [Bengal Presidency](/wiki/Bengal_Presidency "Bengal Presidency") was re-established in 1912 as a governorship. All these provinces had their own legislatures established by the Indian Councils Acts, and high courts established by Indian High Courts Acts. Laws passed by these legislatures needed the dual assent of the governor or lieutenant-governor of the province and the [governor-general of India](/wiki/Governor-general_of_India "Governor-general of India"), who functioned as the representative of the [Emperor](/wiki/Emperor_of_India "Emperor of India").

* There were three governor's provinces in 1912, styled "Presidencies" as a historical memento that they had been once governed by presidents. These were:
  + [Bombay](/wiki/Bombay_Presidency "Bombay Presidency")
  + [Madras](/wiki/Madras_Presidency "Madras Presidency") (officially Presidency of Fort St. George)
  + [Bengal](/wiki/Bengal_Presidency "Bengal Presidency") (from 1912)
* There were six lieutenant-governor's provinces in 1905. These were:
  + [Burma](/wiki/British_rule_in_Burma "British rule in Burma")
  + [Punjab](/wiki/Punjab_Province_(1849%E2%80%931947) "Punjab Province (1849–1947)")
  + [Central Provinces](/wiki/Central_Provinces "Central Provinces")
  + [Bengal](/wiki/Bengal_Presidency "Bengal Presidency") (till 1912)
  + [Eastern Bengal and Assam](/wiki/Eastern_Bengal_and_Assam "Eastern Bengal and Assam") (1905–1912)
  + [Agra and Oudh](/wiki/Agra_Province "Agra Province")
    - [North-Western Provinces and Oudh](/wiki/North-Western_Provinces_and_Oudh "North-Western Provinces and Oudh") (1878–1902)
    - [United Provinces of Agra and Oudh](/wiki/United_Provinces_of_Agra_and_Oudh "United Provinces of Agra and Oudh") (1902–1935)

There were territories ruled directly by the Government of India through nominated chief commissioners. These were former independent states annexed to India and since ruled directly by the Supreme Government.

* There were the three chief commissioner's provinces. These did not have a legislature or a high court. These were:
  + [Ajmer-Merwara](/wiki/Ajmer-Merwara "Ajmer-Merwara")
  + [Coorg](/wiki/Coorg_Province "Coorg Province")
  + [Oudh](/wiki/List_of_chief_commissioners_of_Oudh "List of chief commissioners of Oudh") (till 1878)
  + [Delhi](/wiki/Delhi "Delhi") (from 1911, capital of India)

The vast majority of the Indian states in the late nineteenth century were, in terms of imperial divisions, organised within the provinces. A good number of states were organised into imperial structures called agencies, or residencies. An Agent to the Governor-General (AGG) functioned as the Emperor's representative to all the states in the agency.

* At the time of establishment of the empire, there were only two agencies:
  + [Rajputana](/wiki/Rajputana_Agency "Rajputana Agency")
  + [Central India](/wiki/Central_India_Agency "Central India Agency")
* Several kingdoms were neither part of a province nor an agency; they each had direct relations with the Emperor:
  + [Kashmir and Jammu](/wiki/Jammu_and_Kashmir_(union_territory) "Jammu and Kashmir (union territory)")
  + [Nizamate of Hyderabad](/wiki/Nizamate_of_Hyderabad "Nizamate of Hyderabad")
  + [Kingdom of Mysore](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Mysore "Kingdom of Mysore")

### 1919–1935

In 1919, the fourth [Government of India](/wiki/Government_of_India_Act_1919 "Government of India Act 1919") was enacted by the Crown. This saw many major changes. The legislatures of the provinces were made elected ones rather than nominated ones. Some provinces were given [bicameral legislatures](/wiki/Bicameral_legislature "Bicameral legislature"). All provinces were elevated to governorships and all lieutenant governors were made governors. Burma was given a special status and made an autonomous province.

* There were ten governor's provinces now. These were:
  + [Bombay](/wiki/Bombay_Presidency "Bombay Presidency")
  + [Madras](/wiki/Madras_Presidency "Madras Presidency")
  + [Bengal](/wiki/Bengal_Presidency "Bengal Presidency")
  + [Burma](/wiki/British_rule_in_Burma "British rule in Burma")
  + [Punjab](/wiki/Punjab_Province_(1849%E2%80%931947) "Punjab Province (1849–1947)")
  + [Central Provinces and Berar](/wiki/Central_Provinces_and_Berar "Central Provinces and Berar")
  + [United Provinces](/wiki/United_Provinces_of_British_India "United Provinces of British India")
  + [North-West Frontier Province](/wiki/North-West_Frontier_Province "North-West Frontier Province")
  + [Assam](/wiki/Assam_Province "Assam Province")
  + [Bihar and Orissa](/wiki/Bihar_and_Orissa "Bihar and Orissa")
* There were four chief commissioner's provinces. Their status mostly remained unchanged. These were:
  + [Ajmer-Merwara](/wiki/Ajmer-Merwara "Ajmer-Merwara")
  + [Coorg](/wiki/Coorg_Province "Coorg Province")
  + [Balochistan](/wiki/Chief_Commissioner%27s_Province_of_Balochistan "Chief Commissioner's Province of Balochistan")
  + [Delhi](/wiki/Delhi "Delhi")

The [Chamber of Princes](/wiki/Chamber_of_Princes "Chamber of Princes") was established by [Emperor George V](/wiki/Emperor_George_V "Emperor George V") in 1920. One of the major consequences of this was the creation of many more agencies from the states of the provinces, granting them direct relations with the Emperor instead of with the Governors.

* There were now 8 imperial agencies and residencies. These were:
  + [Punjab States](/wiki/Punjab_States_Agency "Punjab States Agency") (formerly part of Punjab Province)
  + [Madras States](/wiki/Madras_States_Agency "Madras States Agency") (formerly part of Madras Presidency)
  + [Deccan States Agency and Kolhapur Residency](/wiki/Deccan_States_Agency_and_Kolhapur_Residency "Deccan States Agency and Kolhapur Residency") (formerly part of Bombay Presidency)
  + [Western India and Gujarat States Agency and Baroda Residency](/wiki/Western_India_and_Gujarat_States_Agency_and_Baroda_Residency "Western India and Gujarat States Agency and Baroda Residency") (formerly part of Bombay Presidency)
  + [Eastern States](/wiki/Eastern_States_Agency "Eastern States Agency") (formerly part of Bengal Presidency, Bihar and Orissa Province and Central Provinces)
  + [Gwalior Residency](/wiki/Gwalior_Residency "Gwalior Residency") (formerly part of [Central India Agency](/wiki/Central_India_Agency "Central India Agency"))
  + [Rajputana](/wiki/Rajputana_Agency "Rajputana Agency")
  + [Central India](/wiki/Central_India_Agency "Central India Agency")

This saw the separation of all the states from the provinces and addition to before-mentioned agencies. This left all the provinces with only territories under direct Crown rule.

* Several states continued their direct relations with the Emperor through this period, including:
  + [Kashmir and Jammu](/wiki/Jammu_and_Kashmir_(princely_state) "Jammu and Kashmir (princely state)")
  + [Nizamate of Hyderabad](/wiki/Nizamate_of_Hyderabad "Nizamate of Hyderabad")
  + [Kingdom of Mysore](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Mysore "Kingdom of Mysore")

### 1935–1947

The latter years of the [Indian Empire](/wiki/British_Raj "British Raj") saw the enactment of the last [Government of India Act](/wiki/Government_of_India_Act_1935 "Government of India Act 1935") by the Crown. This act granted full autonomy to Indian provinces. Provincial laws no longer needed the assent of the governor-general. This act created the office of a [Premier](/wiki/Premier "Premier") in each province, who functioned as the new head of government and was responsible to the provincial legislature.

Bengal, Madras and Bombay which had been till now styled Presidencies, were now officially styled as provinces. The provinces of [Orissa](/wiki/Orissa_province "Orissa province") and [Sind](/wiki/Sind_province "Sind province") were created from Bihar and Bombay respectively. The [Province of Burma](/wiki/Province_of_Burma "Province of Burma") which had previously functioned as an autonomous province of India was now separated from the Indian Empire, and established as the [Crown Colony of Burma](/wiki/Crown_Colony_of_Burma "Crown Colony of Burma").

* The new set of 12 governor's provinces were:
  + [Bombay](/wiki/Bombay_Presidency "Bombay Presidency")
  + [Sind](/wiki/Sind_Province "Sind Province")
  + [Madras](/wiki/Madras_Presidency "Madras Presidency")
  + [Bengal](/wiki/Bengal_Presidency "Bengal Presidency")
  + [Burma](/wiki/British_rule_in_Burma "British rule in Burma")
  + [Punjab](/wiki/Punjab_Province_(1849%E2%80%931947) "Punjab Province (1849–1947)")
  + [Central Provinces and Berar](/wiki/Central_Provinces_and_Berar "Central Provinces and Berar")
  + [United Provinces](/wiki/United_Provinces_(1937%E2%80%931950) "United Provinces (1937–1950)")
  + [North-West Frontier Province](/wiki/North-West_Frontier_Province "North-West Frontier Province")
  + [Assam](/wiki/Assam_Province "Assam Province")
  + [Bihar](/wiki/Bihar_Province "Bihar Province")
  + [Orissa](/wiki/Orissa_Province "Orissa Province")
* There were 4 chief commissioner's provinces:
  + [Ajmer-Merwara](/wiki/Ajmer-Merwara "Ajmer-Merwara")
  + [Coorg](/wiki/Coorg_Province "Coorg Province")
  + [Balochistan](/wiki/Chief_Commissioner%27s_Province_of_Balochistan "Chief Commissioner's Province of Balochistan")
  + [Delhi](/wiki/Delhi "Delhi")
* There were 8 imperial agencies and residencies:
  + [Punjab States](/wiki/Punjab_States_Agency "Punjab States Agency")
  + [Madras States](/wiki/Madras_States_Agency "Madras States Agency")
  + [Deccan States Agency and Kolhapur Residency](/wiki/Deccan_States_Agency_and_Kolhapur_Residency "Deccan States Agency and Kolhapur Residency")
  + [Western India and Gujarat States Agency and Baroda Residency](/wiki/Western_India_and_Gujarat_States_Agency_and_Baroda_Residency "Western India and Gujarat States Agency and Baroda Residency")
  + [Eastern States](/wiki/Eastern_States_Agency "Eastern States Agency")
  + [Gwalior Residency](/wiki/Gwalior_Residency "Gwalior Residency")
  + [Rajputana](/wiki/Rajputana_Agency "Rajputana Agency")
  + [Central India](/wiki/Central_India_Agency "Central India Agency")
* Three kingdoms were in direct relations with the Emperor.

In 1947, the last Act of the Crown was passed. The act dissolved the Indian Empire, the [Imperial Legislative Council](/wiki/Imperial_Legislative_Council "Imperial Legislative Council") and the [Chamber of Princes](/wiki/Chamber_of_Princes "Chamber of Princes"). The [Union of India](/wiki/Dominion_of_India "Dominion of India") was consequently established from 9 former Indian provinces ([East Punjab](/wiki/East_Punjab "East Punjab"), [United Provinces](/wiki/United_Provinces_(1937%E2%80%931950) "United Provinces (1937–1950)"), [Central Provinces](/wiki/Central_Provinces "Central Provinces"), [Madras](/wiki/Madras_Province "Madras Province"), [Bombay](/wiki/Bombay_Province "Bombay Province"), [Bihar](/wiki/Bihar "Bihar"), [Orissa](/wiki/Orissa "Orissa"), [West Bengal](/wiki/West_Bengal "West Bengal") and [Assam](/wiki/Assam "Assam")) and 562 former Indian states.

### 1947–1950

Main articles: [Political integration of India](/wiki/Political_integration_of_India "Political integration of India"), [Instrument of Accession](/wiki/Instrument_of_Accession "Instrument of Accession"), [Annexation of Junagadh](/wiki/Annexation_of_Junagadh "Annexation of Junagadh"), and [Annexation of Hyderabad](/wiki/Annexation_of_Hyderabad "Annexation of Hyderabad")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/00/1950_India_showing_Political_Divisions_in_the_New_Republic_by_Survey_of_India.jpg/250px-1950_India_showing_Political_Divisions_in_the_New_Republic_by_Survey_of_India.jpg)](/wiki/File:1950_India_showing_Political_Divisions_in_the_New_Republic_by_Survey_of_India.jpg)

The administrative divisions of the Union of India in 1950.

Between 1947 and 1950, the territories of the princely states were politically integrated into the new [Indian Union](/wiki/Political_integration_of_India "Political integration of India"). Most were merged into existing provinces. Others were organised into new provinces and [states](/wiki/Rajpramukh "Rajpramukh"), such as [Rajasthan](/wiki/Rajasthan "Rajasthan"), [Himachal Pradesh](/wiki/Himachal_Pradesh "Himachal Pradesh"), [Malwa Union](/wiki/Malwa_Union "Malwa Union"), [Baghelkhand and Bundelkhand States Union](/wiki/Vindhya_Pradesh "Vindhya Pradesh"), and [Patiala and East Punjab States Union](/wiki/Patiala_and_East_Punjab_States_Union "Patiala and East Punjab States Union"), made up of multiple princely states. A few, including [Mysore](/wiki/Mysore_State "Mysore State"), [Hyderabad](/wiki/Hyderabad_State_(1948%E2%80%9356) "Hyderabad State (1948–56)"), [Bhopal](/wiki/Bhopal_State_(1949%E2%80%9356) "Bhopal State (1949–56)"), and [Bilaspur](/wiki/Bilaspur_State_(1950%E2%80%9354) "Bilaspur State (1950–54)"), became separate states.[[3]](#cite_note-Consti-4)

The new [Constitution of India](/wiki/Constitution_of_India "Constitution of India"), which came into force on 26 January 1950, made India a sovereign democratic republic. The new republic was also declared to be a "Union of States". The constitution of 1950 distinguished between three main types of states:[[3]](#cite_note-Consti-4)

* Part A states, which were the former governors' provinces of India, were ruled by an elected governor and state legislature. The nine Part A states were:
  + [Assam](/wiki/Assam "Assam") (formerly [Assam Province](/wiki/Assam_Province "Assam Province")),
  + [Bihar](/wiki/Bihar "Bihar") (formerly [Bihar Province](/wiki/Bihar_Province "Bihar Province")),
  + [Bombay](/wiki/Bombay_State "Bombay State") (formerly [Bombay Province](/wiki/Bombay_Presidency "Bombay Presidency")),
  + [East Punjab](/wiki/East_Punjab "East Punjab") (formerly [Punjab Province](/wiki/Punjab_Province_(British_India) "Punjab Province (British India)")),
  + [Madhya Pradesh](/wiki/Madhya_Pradesh "Madhya Pradesh") (formerly the [Central Provinces and Berar](/wiki/Central_Provinces_and_Berar "Central Provinces and Berar")),
  + [Madras](/wiki/Madras_State "Madras State") (formerly [Madras Province](/wiki/Madras_Presidency "Madras Presidency")),
  + [Orissa](/wiki/Odisha "Odisha") (formerly [Orissa Province](/wiki/Orissa_Province "Orissa Province")),
  + [Uttar Pradesh](/wiki/Uttar_Pradesh "Uttar Pradesh") (formerly the [United Provinces](/wiki/United_Provinces_(1937%E2%80%931950) "United Provinces (1937–1950)")), and
  + [West Bengal](/wiki/West_Bengal "West Bengal") (formerly [Bengal Province](/wiki/Bengal_Presidency "Bengal Presidency")).
* The eight Part B states were former princely states or groups of princely states, governed by a [rajpramukh](/wiki/Rajpramukh "Rajpramukh"), who was usually the ruler of a constituent state, and an elected legislature. The rajpramukh was appointed by the [President of India](/wiki/President_of_India "President of India"). The Part B states were:
  + [Hyderabad](/wiki/Hyderabad_State_(1948%E2%80%9356) "Hyderabad State (1948–56)") (formerly [Hyderabad Princely State](/wiki/Hyderabad_State "Hyderabad State")),
  + [Jammu and Kashmir](/wiki/Jammu_and_Kashmir_(state) "Jammu and Kashmir (state)") (formerly [Jammu and Kashmir Princely State](/wiki/Jammu_and_Kashmir_(princely_state) "Jammu and Kashmir (princely state)")),
  + [Madhya Bharat](/wiki/Madhya_Bharat "Madhya Bharat") (formerly [Central India Agency](/wiki/Central_India_Agency "Central India Agency")),
  + [Mysore](/wiki/Mysore_State "Mysore State") (formerly [Mysore Princely State](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Mysore "Kingdom of Mysore")),
  + [Patiala and East Punjab States Union](/wiki/Patiala_and_East_Punjab_States_Union "Patiala and East Punjab States Union") (PEPSU),
  + [Rajasthan](/wiki/Rajasthan "Rajasthan") (formerly [Rajputana Agency](/wiki/Rajputana_Agency "Rajputana Agency")),
  + [Saurashtra](/wiki/Saurashtra_(state) "Saurashtra (state)") (formerly [Baroda, Western India and Gujarat States Agency](/wiki/Baroda,_Western_India_and_Gujarat_States_Agency "Baroda, Western India and Gujarat States Agency")), and
  + [Travancore–Cochin](/wiki/Travancore%E2%80%93Cochin "Travancore–Cochin") (formerly [Travancore Princely State](/wiki/Travancore "Travancore") and [Cochin Princely State](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Cochin "Kingdom of Cochin")).
* The ten Part C states included both the former chief commissioners' provinces and some princely states, and each was governed by a chief commissioner appointed by the President of India. The Part C states were:
  + [Ajmer](/wiki/Ajmer_State "Ajmer State") (formerly [Ajmer-Merwara Province](/wiki/Ajmer-Merwara "Ajmer-Merwara")),
  + [Bhopal](/wiki/Bhopal_State_(1949%E2%80%9356) "Bhopal State (1949–56)") (formerly [Bhopal Princely State](/wiki/Bhopal_State "Bhopal State")),
  + [Bilaspur](/wiki/Bilaspur_State_(1950%E2%80%9354) "Bilaspur State (1950–54)") (formerly [Bilaspur Princely State](/wiki/Bilaspur_State_(princely_state) "Bilaspur State (princely state)")),
  + [Coorg State](/wiki/Coorg_State "Coorg State") (formerly [Coorg Province](/wiki/Coorg_Province "Coorg Province")),
  + [Delhi](/wiki/Delhi "Delhi"),
  + [Himachal Pradesh](/wiki/Himachal_Pradesh "Himachal Pradesh"),
  + [Kutch](/wiki/Kutch_State "Kutch State") (formerly [Cutch Princely State](/wiki/Cutch_State "Cutch State")),
  + [Manipur](/wiki/Manipur "Manipur") (formerly [Manipur Princely State](/wiki/Manipur_(princely_state) "Manipur (princely state)")),
  + [Tripura](/wiki/Tripura "Tripura") (formerly [Tripura Princely State](/wiki/Tripura_(princely_state) "Tripura (princely state)")), and
  + [Vindhya Pradesh](/wiki/Vindhya_Pradesh "Vindhya Pradesh") (formerly [Central India Agency](/wiki/Central_India_Agency "Central India Agency")).
* The only Part D state was the [Andaman and Nicobar Islands](/wiki/Andaman_and_Nicobar_Islands "Andaman and Nicobar Islands"), which were administered by a lieutenant governor appointed by the union government.

### States reorganisation (1951–1956)

Main articles: [Goa liberation movement](/wiki/Goa_liberation_movement "Goa liberation movement"), [Andhra movement](/wiki/Andhra_movement "Andhra movement"), [Annexation of Dadra and Nagar Haveli](/wiki/Annexation_of_Dadra_and_Nagar_Haveli "Annexation of Dadra and Nagar Haveli"), and [States Reorganisation Commission](/wiki/States_Reorganisation_Commission "States Reorganisation Commission")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/71/Indien_1955.svg/330px-Indien_1955.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Indien_1955.svg)

India before the States Reorganisation Act, 1956.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/95/Indien_1959.svg/330px-Indien_1959.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Indien_1959.svg)

India after the States Reorganisation Act

[Andhra State](/wiki/Andhra_State "Andhra State") was created on 1 October 1953 from the [Telugu](/wiki/Telugu_language "Telugu language")-speaking northern [districts](/wiki/Districts_of_India "Districts of India") of [Madras State](/wiki/Madras_State "Madras State").[[4]](#cite_note-5)

The [French enclave](/wiki/French_India "French India") of [Chandernagore](/wiki/Chandannagar "Chandannagar") was transferred to [West Bengal](/wiki/West_Bengal "West Bengal") in 1954. In the same year [Pondicherry](/wiki/Puducherry_(city) "Puducherry (city)"), comprising the former French enclaves of [Pondichéry](/wiki/Pondicherry_district "Pondicherry district"), [Karikal](/wiki/Karaikal_district "Karaikal district"), [Yanaon](/wiki/Yanam_district "Yanam district") and [Mahé](/wiki/Mah%C3%A9_district "Mahé district"), was transferred to India. This became a union territory in 1962.[[5]](#cite_note-6)

Also in 1954, pro-India forces liberated the [Portuguese-held enclaves](/wiki/Portuguese_India "Portuguese India") of [Dadrá](/wiki/Dadra,_Dadra_and_Nagar_Haveli_and_Daman_and_Diu "Dadra, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu") and [Nagar Aveli](/wiki/Nagar_Haveli "Nagar Haveli"), declaring the short-lived *[de facto](/wiki/De_facto "De facto")* state of [Free Dadra and Nagar Haveli](/wiki/Free_Dadra_and_Nagar_Haveli "Free Dadra and Nagar Haveli"). In 1961, India annexed it as the Union Territory of [Dadra and Nagar Haveli](/wiki/Dadra_and_Nagar_Haveli "Dadra and Nagar Haveli").[[6]](#cite_note-7)[[7]](#cite_note-8)[[8]](#cite_note-9)[[9]](#cite_note-10)

The [States Reorganisation Act, 1956](/wiki/States_Reorganisation_Act,_1956 "States Reorganisation Act, 1956") reorganised the states based on linguistic lines resulting in the creation of the new states.[[3]](#cite_note-Consti-4)

As a result of this act:

* [Madras State](/wiki/Madras_State "Madras State") retained its name, with [Kanyakumari district](/wiki/Kanyakumari_district "Kanyakumari district") added to form [Travancore–Cochin](/wiki/Travancore%E2%80%93Cochin "Travancore–Cochin").
* [Andhra Pradesh](/wiki/Andhra_Pradesh "Andhra Pradesh") was created with the merger of [Andhra State](/wiki/Andhra_State "Andhra State") with the [Telugu](/wiki/Telugu_language "Telugu language")-speaking districts of [Hyderabad State](/wiki/Hyderabad_State "Hyderabad State") in 1956.
* [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala") was created by merging [Malabar district](/wiki/Malabar_district "Malabar district") and the [Kasaragod taluk](/wiki/Kasaragod_taluk "Kasaragod taluk") of [South Canara](/wiki/South_Canara "South Canara") districts of [Madras State](/wiki/Madras_State "Madras State") with [Travancore–Cochin](/wiki/Travancore%E2%80%93Cochin "Travancore–Cochin").
* [Mysore State](/wiki/Mysore_State "Mysore State") was re-organised with the addition of the districts of [Bellary](/wiki/Bellary_district "Bellary district") and [South Canara](/wiki/South_Canara "South Canara") (excluding [Kasaragod taluk](/wiki/Kasaragod_taluk "Kasaragod taluk")) and the [Kollegal taluk](/wiki/Kollegal "Kollegal") of [Coimbatore district](/wiki/Coimbatore_district "Coimbatore district") from the Madras State, the districts of [Belgaum](/wiki/Belgaum_district "Belgaum district"), [Bijapur](/wiki/Bijapur_district,_Karnataka "Bijapur district, Karnataka"), [North Canara](/wiki/North_Canara "North Canara") and [Dharwad](/wiki/Dharwad_district "Dharwad district") from [Bombay State](/wiki/Bombay_State "Bombay State"), the [Kannada](/wiki/Kannada "Kannada")-majority districts of [Bidar](/wiki/Bidar_district "Bidar district"), [Raichur](/wiki/Raichur_district "Raichur district") and [Kalaburagi](/wiki/Kalaburagi_district "Kalaburagi district") from [Hyderabad State](/wiki/Hyderabad_State_(1948%E2%80%9356) "Hyderabad State (1948–56)") and the [Coorg State](/wiki/Coorg_State "Coorg State").
* The [Laccadive Islands](/wiki/Laccadive_Islands "Laccadive Islands"), [Aminidivi Islands](/wiki/Aminidivi "Aminidivi") and [Minicoy Island](/wiki/Minicoy "Minicoy"), which had been divided between the South Canara and Malabar districts of Madras State, were united and organised into the union territory of [Lakshadweep](/wiki/Lakshadweep "Lakshadweep").
* [Bombay State](/wiki/Bombay_State "Bombay State") was enlarged by the addition of [Saurashtra State](/wiki/Saurashtra_(state) "Saurashtra (state)") and [Kutch State](/wiki/Kutch_State "Kutch State"), the [Marathi](/wiki/Marathi_language "Marathi language")-speaking districts of [Nagpur division](/wiki/Nagpur_division "Nagpur division") of [Madhya Pradesh](/wiki/Madhya_Pradesh "Madhya Pradesh") and the [Marathwada](/wiki/Marathwada "Marathwada") region of [Hyderabad State](/wiki/Hyderabad_State_(1948%E2%80%9356) "Hyderabad State (1948–56)").
* [Rajasthan](/wiki/Rajasthan "Rajasthan") and [Punjab](/wiki/Punjab,_India "Punjab, India") gained territories from [Ajmer State](/wiki/Ajmer_State "Ajmer State") and [Patiala and East Punjab States Union](/wiki/Patiala_and_East_Punjab_States_Union "Patiala and East Punjab States Union") respectively and certain territories of [Bihar](/wiki/Bihar "Bihar") were transferred to [West Bengal](/wiki/West_Bengal "West Bengal").

### Post-1956

Main articles: [Mahagujarat movement](/wiki/Mahagujarat_movement "Mahagujarat movement"), [Punjabi Suba movement](/wiki/Punjabi_Suba_movement "Punjabi Suba movement"), [Annexation of Goa](/wiki/Annexation_of_Goa "Annexation of Goa"), [1967 Goa status referendum](/wiki/1967_Goa_status_referendum "1967 Goa status referendum"), [1975 Sikkimese monarchy referendum](/wiki/1975_Sikkimese_monarchy_referendum "1975 Sikkimese monarchy referendum"), [Uttarakhand movement](/wiki/Uttarakhand_movement "Uttarakhand movement"), and [Telangana movement](/wiki/Telangana_movement "Telangana movement")

Bombay State was split into the linguistic states of [Gujarat](/wiki/Gujarat "Gujarat") and [Maharashtra](/wiki/Maharashtra "Maharashtra") on 1 May 1960 by the Bombay Reorganisation Act.[[10]](#cite_note-11) The former Union Territory of [Nagaland](/wiki/Nagaland "Nagaland") achieved statehood on 1 December 1963.[[11]](#cite_note-12) The [Punjab Reorganisation Act, 1966](/wiki/Punjab_Reorganisation_Act,_1966 "Punjab Reorganisation Act, 1966") resulted in the creation of [Haryana](/wiki/Haryana "Haryana") on 1 November and the transfer of the northern districts of Punjab to [Himachal Pradesh](/wiki/Himachal_Pradesh "Himachal Pradesh").[[12]](#cite_note-13) The act designated [Chandigarh](/wiki/Chandigarh "Chandigarh") as a union territory and the shared capital of Punjab and Haryana.[[13]](#cite_note-14)

Madras State was renamed [Tamil Nadu](/wiki/Tamil_Nadu "Tamil Nadu") in 1969. The north-eastern states of [Manipur](/wiki/Manipur "Manipur"), [Meghalaya](/wiki/Meghalaya "Meghalaya") and [Tripura](/wiki/Tripura "Tripura") were formed on 21 January 1972.[[14]](#cite_note-15) Mysore State was renamed [Karnataka](/wiki/Karnataka "Karnataka") in 1973. On 16 May 1975, [Sikkim](/wiki/Sikkim "Sikkim") became the 22nd state of the Indian Union and the [state's monarchy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Sikkim "Kingdom of Sikkim") was abolished.[[15]](#cite_note-16) In 1987, [Arunachal Pradesh](/wiki/Arunachal_Pradesh "Arunachal Pradesh") and [Mizoram](/wiki/Mizoram "Mizoram") became states on 20 February, followed by [Goa](/wiki/Goa "Goa") on 30 May, while erstwhile union territory of [Goa, Daman and Diu](/wiki/Goa,_Daman_and_Diu "Goa, Daman and Diu")'s northern [exclaves](/wiki/Exclave "Exclave") [Damão](/wiki/Daman_district,_India "Daman district, India") and [Diu](/wiki/Diu_district "Diu district") became a separate union territory as [Daman and Diu](/wiki/Daman_and_Diu "Daman and Diu").[[16]](#cite_note-17)

In November 2000, three new states were created, namely:

* [Chhattisgarh](/wiki/Chhattisgarh "Chhattisgarh"), from eastern [Madhya Pradesh](/wiki/Madhya_Pradesh "Madhya Pradesh"),
* Uttaranchal, from northwest [Uttar Pradesh](/wiki/Uttar_Pradesh "Uttar Pradesh") (renamed [Uttarakhand](/wiki/Uttarakhand "Uttarakhand") in 2007), and
* [Jharkhand](/wiki/Jharkhand "Jharkhand"), from southern districts of Bihar with the enforcement of [Madhya Pradesh Reorganisation Act, 2000](/wiki/Madhya_Pradesh_Reorganisation_Act,_2000 "Madhya Pradesh Reorganisation Act, 2000"), [Uttar Pradesh Reorganisation Act, 2000](/wiki/Uttar_Pradesh_Reorganisation_Act,_2000 "Uttar Pradesh Reorganisation Act, 2000") and [Bihar Reorganisation Act, 2000](/wiki/Bihar_Reorganisation_Act,_2000 "Bihar Reorganisation Act, 2000") respectively.[[17]](#cite_note-18)[[18]](#cite_note-19)[[19]](#cite_note-20)[[20]](#cite_note-21)

Pondicherry was renamed [Puducherry](/wiki/Puducherry_(union_territory) "Puducherry (union territory)") in 2007 and Orissa was renamed [Odisha](/wiki/Odisha "Odisha") in 2011. [Telangana](/wiki/Telangana "Telangana") was created on 2 June 2014 from ten former districts of north-western [Andhra Pradesh](/wiki/Andhra_Pradesh "Andhra Pradesh").[[21]](#cite_note-APT-22)[[22]](#cite_note-23)

In August 2019, the Parliament of India passed the [Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act, 2019](/wiki/Jammu_and_Kashmir_Reorganisation_Act,_2019 "Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act, 2019"), which contains provisions to reorganise the state of [Jammu and Kashmir](/wiki/Jammu_and_Kashmir_(state) "Jammu and Kashmir (state)") into two union territories; [Jammu and Kashmir](/wiki/Jammu_and_Kashmir_(union_territory) "Jammu and Kashmir (union territory)") and [Ladakh](/wiki/Ladakh "Ladakh"), effective from 31 October 2019.[[23]](#cite_note-24) Later that year in November, the Government of India introduced legislation to merge the union territories of [Daman and Diu](/wiki/Daman_and_Diu "Daman and Diu") and [Dadra and Nagar Haveli](/wiki/Dadra_and_Nagar_Haveli "Dadra and Nagar Haveli") into a single union territory to be known as [Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu](/wiki/Dadra_and_Nagar_Haveli_and_Daman_and_Diu "Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu"), effective from 26 January 2020.[[24]](#cite_note-25)[[25]](#cite_note-26)[[26]](#cite_note-27)

3. **[^](#cite_ref-48)** Andhra Pradesh was divided into two states, [Telangana](/wiki/Telangana "Telangana") and a residual [Andhra Pradesh](/wiki/Andhra_Pradesh "Andhra Pradesh") on 2 June 2014.[[43]](#cite_note-The_Times_of_India-45)[[21]](#cite_note-APT-22) [Hyderabad](/wiki/Hyderabad,_India "Hyderabad, India"), located entirely within the borders of Telangana, is to serve as the capital for both states for a period of time not exceeding ten years.[[44]](#cite_note-46) The Government of Andhra Pradesh and the Andhra Pradesh Legislature completed the process of relocating to temporary facilities in the envisaged new capital city [Amaravati](/wiki/Amaravati "Amaravati") in early 2017.[[45]](#cite_note-47)

5. **[^](#cite_ref-54)** Hindi, Odia, Punjabi, Santali, Urdu, Kamatapuri, Rajbanshi, Kurmali and Kurukh are treated as additional official languages in blocks, divisions or districts where the speakers exceed 10% of the population.

## Responsibilities and authorities

Main articles: [Federalism in India](/wiki/Federalism_in_India "Federalism in India"), [Union List](/wiki/Union_List "Union List"), [State List](/wiki/State_List "State List"), and [Concurrent List](/wiki/Concurrent_List "Concurrent List")

The [Constitution of India](/wiki/Constitution_of_India "Constitution of India") distributes the sovereign executive and legislative powers exercisable with respect to the territory of any state between the Union Government and that state's own State Government.[[63]](#cite_note-68)

["Territoriality of executive powers of states in India"](https://web.archive.org/web/20091231013230/http://www.ebc-india.com/lawyer/articles/9801a2.htm). *Constitutional Law*. Archived from [the original](http://www.ebc-india.com/lawyer/articles/9801a2.htm) on 31 December 2009. "Article 73 broadly stated, provides that the executive power of the Union shall extend to the matters with respect to which Parliament has power to make laws. Article 162 similarly provides that the executive power of a State shall extend to the matters with respect to which the Legislature of a State has power to make laws. The Supreme Court has reiterated this position when it ruled in the *Ramanaiah case* that the executive power of the Union or of the State broadly speaking, is [coextensive](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/coextensive "wikt:coextensive") and [coterminous](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/coterminous "wikt:coterminous") with its respective legislative power"
